request: Make the harness auto-resume after each consent gate (/approve-spec, /approve-swarm, /grant-commit, /grant-push) when a workflow is armed. Today, after the user runs a consent slash command, the harness loop has yielded and the user must type /harness again to continue. Three candidate design surfaces: (a) the consent slash command body chains into Skill(harness) after writing its token; (b) the harness_continuation Stop hook fires on a fresh consent token; (c) the consent_gate_grant hook additionally arms a 'harness wants to resume' signal that another lifecycle hook picks up. Verbatim from user (2026-05-17): 'after each consent gate if the harness is armed we shouldn't ask the user to run harness again or ask them to continue...' Cross-cutting impact: touches consent-gate hook, Stop hook, possibly all 4 consent command bodies, and Article V wording. Security-relevant: consent gates are a primitive in the workflow's safety model.
